probe_designs.content: Run scib_metrics.kBET on the scVI latent embedding, stratified by the 'batch' covariate. For each batch-specific neighborhood, kBET tests whether local kNN neighborhoods have batch compositions consistent with the global batch ratio. Healthy: mean kBET acceptance rate >= 0.8 with per-batch acceptance rate variance < 0.1. Unhealthy: acceptance near 0 (over-correction / miscalibrated null) or below 0.5 (batch effect remains).
possible_sources: Büttner et al. 2019 'A test metric for assessing single-cell RNA-seq batch correction' (kBET)
scib_metrics.kBET implementation
Luecken et al. 2022 'Benchmarking atlas-level data integration in single-cell genomics' (scIB)

probe_designs.content: Compute scib_metrics.ilisi_graph and clisi_graph on the latent kNN graph. iLISI measures how mixed batches are in local neighborhoods (higher = more mixed, good); cLISI measures how mixed cell-types are (lower = better bio-preservation). Healthy: iLISI close to max (n_batches=2) AND cLISI close to 1. Unhealthy: iLISI=1 (no mixing) or cLISI=2 (bio-structure destroyed by over-integration). Report both together to detect over-correction.
possible_sources: Korsunsky et al. 2019 'Fast, sensitive and accurate integration of single-cell data with Harmony' (LISI)
scib_metrics.ilisi_graph / clisi_graph
Luecken et al. 2022 scIB benchmark

probe_designs.content: Use scib_metrics.graph_connectivity on the latent kNN graph restricted to each cell-type label. Connectivity = 1 means cells of the same type form a single connected component across both batches (bio-structure intact after integration). Healthy: graph_connectivity >= 0.9 for every cell-type label. Unhealthy: < 0.5 for any type indicates the integration fragmented a cell-type into batch-separated subclusters.
possible_sources: Luecken et al. 2022 scIB (graph_connectivity metric)
scib_metrics.graph_connectivity
Traag et al. 2019 'From Louvain to Leiden' (connected-component / community foundation)

probe_designs.content: Run scib_metrics.asw_label and scib_metrics.asw_label_batch on the latent space. asw_label measures cell-type silhouette width (higher = tighter bio clusters, good); asw_label_batch computes per-batch silhouette and averages 1 - |0.5 - score| to penalize batch isolation. Healthy: asw_label >= 0.6 and asw_label_batch >= 0.7. Unhealthy: asw_label < 0.4 (bio-structure lost) or asw_label_batch < 0.5 (silhouette dominated by batch).
possible_sources: Luecken et al. 2022 scIB (ASW_label, ASW_label_batch)
scib_metrics.asw_label / asw_label_batch
Rosenberg et al. 2018 'OpenProblems' single-cell ASW formulation

probe_designs.content: Use scib_metrics.isolated_labels_f1 with a kNN classifier (k=5) trained on the latent embedding, predicting the cell-type label. Isolated labels are those rare cell-types that are geographically isolated (present in only one batch or unique). F1 here measures how well the latent space preserves hard-to-separate biological labels. Healthy: isolated-label F1 >= 0.7. Unhealthy: <= 0.4 indicates the integration smeared out rare cell-type signal.
possible_sources: Luecken et al. 2022 scIB (isolated_labels_F1)
scib_metrics.isolated_labels_f1
Abdelaal et al. 2019 'Comparison of automated cell-type identification methods for single-cell transcriptomics'

probe_designs.content: Train scVI with one batch held out of the encoder posterior (use scVI's batch as covariate but mask 20% of cells for ELBO eval). Report held-out negative ELBO vs in-distribution ELBO gap. Healthy: gap < 10% of the in-distribution ELBO magnitude. Unhealthy: large gap indicates overfitting to batch-specific noise rather than learning a shared latent manifold.
possible_sources: Lopez et al. 2018 'Deep generative modeling for single-cell transcriptomics' (scVI, ELBO)
Lopez et al. 2018 'Multi-resolution scVI' held-out evaluation
scvi-tools train() ELBO validation

probe_designs.probe_name: Posterior predictive decoupling from library size
probe_designs.content: Sample posterior predictive counts from the scVI decoder and regress decoded total counts against observed per-cell library size and against batch id. Compute partial R². Healthy: posterior predictive mean correlates with observed library size (R² > 0.5) but is independent of batch id given library size (partial R² < 0.05). Unhealthy: batch drives predictive counts → batch leakage into decoder.
possible_sources: Lopez et al. 2018 scVI (decoder generative model w/ library size factor)
Svensson et al. 2020 'Interpretable factor models for single-cell expression'
Yip et al. 2017 'Bayesian approaches for single-cell transcriptomics'

probe_designs.content: Subsample 80% of cells 5 times with fixed seed, recompute the scib_metrics.ilisi_graph and graph_connectivity each run, and measure coefficient of variation (CV). Healthy: CV of iLISI < 5% and CV of graph_connectivity < 3%. Unhealthy: high CV indicates the integration result is fragile to which cells were sampled, i.e., the latent manifold is unstable.
possible_sources: Luecken et al. 2022 scIB (stability protocol)
scib_metrics repeated-run evaluation
Büttner et al. 2019 kBET subsampling robustness

probe_designs.probe_name: Highly variable gene overlap across batches
probe_designs.content: Before/after integration, compute per-batch highly variable genes (scanpy.pp.highly_variable_genes, flavor='seurat_v3', n_top_genes=2000) and report Jaccard overlap of the HVG set between b1 and b2. Healthy: Jaccard >= 0.3 (shared biological signal dominates). Unhealthy: Jaccard < 0.1 means batches measure largely disjoint gene sets — integration quality has an upstream ceiling that the latent space cannot recover from.
possible_sources: Hafemeister & Satija 2019 'Normalization and variance stabilization of single-cell RNA-seq data' (sctransform)
Stuart et al. 2019 'Comprehensive integration of single-cell data' (HVG anchor selection)
Wolf et al. 2018 scanpy HVG

probe_designs.content: Sweep over max_epochs in {20, 50, 100, 200} (or checkpoint every 20 epochs in one run), evaluate scib_metrics.ilisi_graph + asw_label at each. Plot the integration-vs-bio trajectory. Healthy: both iLISI and ASW_label plateau together around the same epoch (efficiency is reached without bio degradation). Unhealthy: iLISI improves only after ASW_label drops → over-correction regime; or neither moves → under-trained.
possible_sources: Lopez et al. 2018 scVI training schedule recommendations
Gayoso et al. 2022 'Deep generative modeling for single-cell transcriptomics with scvi-tools' (training heuristics)
Luecken et al. 2022 scIB epoch-sweep evaluation
